2016-01-25.abstract: The countries’ strategic pact gives China a western gateway to Middle East markets, and has saved Iran from international isolation and economic ruin.

2016-01-25.abstract: The new president, Marcelo Rebelo de Sousa, won 52 percent of the votes, with 98 percent counted.

2016-01-25.title: Protesters in Haiti Demand That President Quit
2016-01-25.abstract: One day earlier, officials had bowed to widespread civil unrest and postponed a single-candidate presidential runoff election that was to be held on Sunday.

2016-01-26.title: Iran Still a Leading Executioner of Minors, Report Says
2016-01-26.abstract: Amnesty International said Iran had executed 73 juvenile offenders since 2005, despite pledging in 1994 to end the death penalty for those under 18.

2016-01-26.title: Pope Francis, Catholics and Lutherans Will Recall Reformation
2016-01-26.abstract: Pope Francis will participate in a joint worship service in Sweden as part of a series of events to commemorate the 500th anniversary of the Reformation.

2016-01-26.title: Yemen: Top Judge and 6 Relatives Are Killed in Airstrike
2016-01-26.abstract: Airstrikes by the Saudi-led military coalition hit the home of a senior judge in Sana, killing the judge along with his relatives.

2016-01-26.title: El Salvador’s Advice on Zika Virus: Don’t Have Babies
2016-01-26.abstract: Grappling with a mosquito-borne virus linked to brain damage in infants, El Salvador is advising all women in the country not to get pregnant until 2018.

2016-01-26.title: Police Presence in Egypt Mutes Most Protests on 5th Anniversary of Uprising
2016-01-26.abstract: Egyptians marked the fifth anniversary of the uprising that toppled President Hosni Mubarak with a handful of flash protests but none of the violence or anger that drove the uprising.

2016-01-27.title: Hundreds Vanishing in Egypt as Crackdown Widens, Activists Say
2016-01-27.abstract: Many suspected of opposing the government have disappeared into secretive detention centers for weeks, rights groups said, and some have later turned up dead.

2016-01-27.title: Zika Testing Is Urged for Some Newborns
2016-01-27.abstract: A range of birth defects, not just small heads, may be linked to infection with the virus, federal health officials said on Tuesday.

2016-01-27.title: How Iran Rejoined the World’s Economy in Just 10 Days
2016-01-27.abstract: President Hassan Rouhani is visiting Europe, meeting the pope, inking business deals and signaling that Iran is again engaging with the world.

2016-01-27.title: Scalping Tickets to the Doctor? Woman in Beijing Has Had Enough
2016-01-27.abstract: A video of a woman denouncing those who resell hospital access for a profit was widely circulated online this week, inciting outrage among many.

2016-01-27.title: Iranian Released in Prisoner Exchange Finds Fault With Its Handling
2016-01-27.abstract: Nader Modanlo, released by the United States this month in a prisoner exchange with Iran, was reluctant to accept the deal because it required him to abandon an appeal of his conviction.

2016-01-28.title: Deprivation in Syria Deepens as U.N. Talks Loom
2016-01-28.abstract: About half a million people are at risk of starving and more areas are cut off from aid, United Nations relief officials say.

2016-01-28.title: Reports of Zika-Linked Birth Defect Rise in Brazil
2016-01-28.abstract: Health authorities said reported cases of microcephaly — a condition in which newborns have abnormally small heads — had climbed to 4,180 since October. Brazil typically has 150 cases annually.

2016-01-28.title: Change of U.S. Command Set in Afghanistan
2016-01-28.abstract: President Obama nominated Lt. Gen. John W. Nicholson Jr. to lead American military operations, replacing Gen. John F. Campbell.

2016-01-28.title: Akira Amari, Japan’s Economic Revitalization Minister, Resigns Amid Scandal
2016-01-28.abstract: The move came after allegations that he took money from the head of a construction company in exchange for political favors.

2016-01-28.title: Sellers of Donkey Gelatin, a Chinese Cure in Crisis, Seek a State Remedy
2016-01-28.abstract: Modern China is breeding fewer donkeys, threatening the supply of a traditional medicine made from their hides and inviting a flood of fakes into the market.

2016-01-29.title: Russia Dismisses German Warnings About Exploiting Teen Rape Claim
2016-01-29.abstract: Russia’s foreign minister said a girl’s disappearance and alleged rape in Berlin was “hushed up.” But German officials have warned against politicizing the case.

2016-01-29.title: China to Try Canadian on Spying Charges
2016-01-29.abstract: Kevin Garratt, who ran a cafe near China’s border with North Korea, will be tried on charges of spying and stealing state secrets nearly a year and a half after he and his wife were detained.

2016-01-29.title: New Details of Iran Ordeal Emerge From Former Prisoners
2016-01-29.abstract: Jason Rezaian of The Washington Post and Matthew Trevithick added to the picture that has been emerging about the prisoner ordeal and the diplomacy that led to their release.

2016-01-29.title: Plotters Talked of Planting Explosives on a Kangaroo in Australia
2016-01-29.abstract: A teenager, who pleaded not guilty to terrorism charges on Thursday, is accused of planning a holiday attack on the police in a Melbourne suburb.

2016-01-29.title: More Is Needed to Beat ISIS, Pentagon Officials Conclude
2016-01-29.abstract: To deal a lasting blow to the extremist Sunni militancy, officials believe that additional forces will be needed to work with Iraqi, Kurdish and Syrian opposition fighters.

2016-01-30.title: France Plans Mideast Peace Effort, and Recognition of Palestine if It Fails
2016-01-30.abstract: The announcement was criticized by Israeli officials who said it would encourage the Palestinians not to negotiate seriously.

2016-01-30.title: Vaccine for Zika Virus May Be Years Away, Disease Experts Warn
2016-01-30.abstract: Not only are scientists still learning about Zika, but any vaccine must go through rigorous testing before it’s available to the public.

2016-01-30.title: Tears and Bewilderment in Brazilian City Facing Zika Crisis
2016-01-30.abstract: The Zika epidemic has spread much faster than science’s understanding of it.

2016-01-30.title: British Woman Is Convicted of Joining ISIS
2016-01-30.abstract: The authorities said Tareena Shakil, who went with her young son to Syria to become a “jihadi bride” but returned to Britain, was the first British woman convicted of joining the militants.

2016-01-30.title: At Some Restaurants in China, a Shortcut to Addictive Food: Poppies
2016-01-30.abstract: At least 36 restaurants across China were investigated in the past week for adding poppy capsules and other illegal ingredients to food, the state news media said.

2016-01-31.title: Greece Resists Its Role as European Union’s Gatekeeper
2016-01-31.abstract: Europe has reached a turning point in the migrant crisis, and is starting to coalesce around proposals that would effectively turn Greece into a giant processing center.

2016-01-31.title: Authorities Arrest Son of Ex-Leader in Sri Lanka
2016-01-31.abstract: Yoshitha Rajapaksa, the second son of former President Mahinda Rajapaksa, was arrested by financial crimes investigators after being questioned over his involvement in alleged money-laundering.

2016-01-31.title: President Xi Jinping of China Is All Business in Middle East Visit
2016-01-31.abstract: President Xi’s arrival after the lifting of sanctions related to Iran’s nuclear program provided a moment for China to set the stage for future deal-making.

2016-01-31.title: Myanmar’s Unemployed Elephants
2016-01-31.abstract: Myanmar’s newly democratic government has banned the export of raw timber, to help fight deforestation. But that has idled thousands of the elephants that toil in the logging industry.

2016-01-31.title: Turkey Accuses Russia of Again Violating Its Airspace
2016-01-31.abstract: Turkey’s Foreign Ministry said a Russian fighter jet entered the country’s airspace after radar units warned it to turn back.
